thread.title: Donald Trump’s Bad Cabinet Picks: Linda McMahon Sued for Allegedly Enabling Abuse of Children
thread.title_full: Donald Trump’s Bad Cabinet Picks: Linda McMahon Sued for Allegedly Enabling Abuse of Children

author: Samyarup Chowdhury
title: Donald Trump’s Bad Cabinet Picks: Linda McMahon Sued for Allegedly Enabling Abuse of Children
text:
Latest Videos
[Donald Trump](https://knewz.com/tag/donald-trump/)'s nomination for Education Secretary, Linda McMahon, has recently become the target of a [lawsuit](https://knewz.com/tag/lawsuit/) that alleges she "knowingly enabled" the sexual exploitation and [abuse](https://knewz.com/tag/child-abuse/) of children by a World Wrestling Entertainment ([WWE](https://knewz.com/tag/wwe/)) employee.
[Knewz.com](https://knewz.com/) has learned that the former WWE CEO has denied the allegations—which reportedly date back to the 1980s.
According to the lawsuit filings, McMahon, her husband Vince – who founded the WWE with his wife Linda – and the WWE, along with its parent company TKO Group Holdings, "allowed" ringside announcer Melvin Phillips Jr. to use his position to sexually exploit children he recruited as "ring boys."
A "ring boy" was tasked with helping Philips Jr. set up and dismantle wrestling rings during events.
However, under the guise of the job, he would sexually abuse the children, often in the presence of wrestlers and executives in the locker area, according to the allegations detailed in the lawsuit.
The lawsuit further alleges that the WWE ringside announcer would often film the abuse he subjected these children to.
The suit was filed on behalf of five individuals who claimed that they were 13 to 15 years old when Philips recruited them as "ring boys" and allegedly subjected them to sexual exploitation and abuse, which left them mentally and emotionally scarred for life.
Philips worked as a “prominent ringside announcer and crew chief” for decades, with his career spanning the 1970s, '80s, and '90s, and according to the lawsuit, his pattern of abuse was "common knowledge" in the WWE circuit.
In fact, Vince and Linda McMahon were at least aware of Philips's “peculiar and unnatural interest” in young boys, the lawsuit alleged.
"Phillips lured and manipulated the young boys with promises of meeting famous wrestlers and attending the highly popular wrestling shows, experiences that were otherwise unattainable for these kids," the filing stated.
"(The McMahons, WWE and TKO Holdings) allowed Phillips and others to engage in, and foster, the WWE’s rampant culture of sexual abuse," the lawsuit further mentioned.
Filed in October, the lawsuit also claims that the McMahons fired Philips in 1988 over the piling allegations of sexual abuse against children. However, he was reportedly rehired by the WWE after only six weeks, under the condition that he "steer clear from kids."
The filing claims that Philips allegedly continued his perverse behavior—this time, with the McMahons completely aware of his alleged crimes.
"It was common knowledge in the WWE — among the ring crew, wrestlers, and executives — that Phillips surrounded himself with a posse of underaged Ring Boys, including when he traveled across state lines and stayed in hotel rooms with the children," the lawsuit alleges.
"After decades of suffering in silence from their childhood trauma, these survivors come forward now to hold Defendants accountable for their conduct in allowing the systemic and pervasive abuse by Philips."
While the allegedly notorious ringside announcer passed away in 2012, the lawsuit aims to hold McMahon and her reportedly estranged husband accountable as "negligent employers."
The five anonymous victims are claiming $30,000 in damages for the alleged abuse they had to endure while working under Philips.
On the other hand, McMahon has denied the allegations, with Laura Brevetti, her attorney, claiming that the allegations are "filled with lies."
"This civil lawsuit based upon thirty-plus year-old allegations is filled with scurrilous lies, exaggerations and misrepresentations regarding Linda McMahon... Ms. McMahon will vigorously defend against this baseless lawsuit and without doubt ultimately succeed," Brevetti [said](https://edition.cnn.com/2024/11/21/business/linda-mcmahon-abuse-wwe-trump-education/index.html) in a statement.
McMahon stepped down as the CEO of WWE in 2009 to run for [Senate](https://knewz.com/tag/senate/) but lost in [Connecticut](https://knewz.com/tag/connecticut/) in 2010 and 2012.
She is currently the co-chair of Trump's "transitional team," according to reports, and was recently nominated by the President-elect for the position of Education Secretary. However, the recent lawsuit has reportedly raised questions about her capability to protect children and act in their best interests.
It is worth noting that Trump's other [cabinet picks](https://knewz.com/health-secretary-pick-rfk-jr-claims-covid-was-ethnically-targeted-to-spare-jews/) have made headlines as well, with his Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth being charged with the [sexual assault](https://knewz.com/tag/sexual-assault/) of a woman in 2017—an incident the Fox News host has described as a [consensual "sexual encounter."](https://knewz.com/police-report-reveals-graphic-details-about-sexual-assault-allegations-against-donald-trumps-defense-secretary-nominee/)
